mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces discount rates and lifts equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns following month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines do not outperform months with stable or rising rates by at least 3% annualized over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the unemployment rate above its trailing 12-month low by more than 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating household income expectations and falling corporate revenue forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE has risen more than 0.3pp above its 12-month low.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and industrial production constitutes a broad-based demand expansion that outpaces supply constraints, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days only when both series jointly accelerate.
failure_condition: The joint acceleration signal of RSAFS and INDPRO produces a 20-day forward return rank correlation below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held above 5 percent, month-over-month increases in consumer prices force the market to discount additional tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production signals improving corporate operating leverage and earnings capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales of more than 1 percent signals a sudden contraction in consumer spending that reverberates through corporate revenue projections, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month RSAFS declines exceeding 1% are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate signal a tightening labor market that boosts wage income and consumption, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when both conditions are met simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases and UNRATE decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of triggered months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent signals a contraction in residential investment that drags down construction employment and durable goods demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines exceeding 5% month-over-month are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.
